faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Rochdale Village apartment?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery, silken tubes or cases in closets, and small beige or gold-colored moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae or cocoons in dark corners of closets or under furniture. Our technicians provide free inspections to confirm clothes moth activity.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth treatment involve in a Rochdale Village co-op?
faqs.answer: Treatment typically includes thorough inspection of all closets and storage areas, targeted application of safe insecticides to affected areas, pheromone traps for monitoring, and detailed recommendations for cleaning and prevention. We coordinate with residents to ensure treatments comply with Rochdale Village co-op guidelines and work around your schedule.

faqs.question: Why does pricing range from $550 to $5,000 for clothes moth control?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the size of your apartment, severity of infestation, number of rooms affected, and whether multiple treatments are needed. A minor infestation in one bedroom closet costs significantly less than a widespread problem affecting multiple rooms and requiring extensive treatment, follow-up visits, and preventive services.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Rochdale Village home?
faqs.answer: Regular vacuuming of closets and under furniture, proper storage of woolens in sealed containers, periodic dry cleaning of seasonal clothing, and maintaining our installed pheromone traps all help prevent reinfestation. We provide detailed prevention guidance and offer ongoing monitoring services to keep your home moth-free year-round.
